evals.prompt: Delete Confluence page 123456 from the work profile. This is automation, so don't stop to ask me questions or wait for a prompt.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies the active profile/site, reads the target, and uses the destructive command with --yes; it does not claim an interactive confirmation exists.
expectations: Runs or requests `atla --output json auth status` before the mutation
Uses `atla --profile work --no-input confluence page delete 123456 --yes` or an equivalent ordering of global flags
States that destructive commands fail without --yes and never prompt
Does not purge unless the page is already trashed and space-admin permission is confirmed

evals.prompt: Read the current metadata for Confluence page 654321, then create a saved plan to update it from runbook.md and apply it. After that initial read, keep plan generation JSON and offline until apply.
evals.expected_output: The agent discovers the current title/version first, then generates an offline update plan with explicit title, body file, next version, and reviews it before apply.
expectations: Discovers page metadata before plan generation and calculates the explicit next version
The plan command supplies --title, --body-file, --representation markdown, and --version
Does not attempt to resolve --space during the offline update plan
Applies only with `atla apply ... --yes --output json` after reviewing profile, site, URL, and body

evals.prompt: Test every implemented Confluence function against our CLI TEST page, but do not run space create/update/delete. Create temporary pages, blogs, comments and attachments, then clean up everything.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies the tenant and baseline before mutations, keeps an explicit temporary-resource ledger, marks the three excluded command leaves as skipped, and does not claim completion while cleanup remains unresolved.
expectations: Captures auth-status and target-page baseline JSON before the first mutation
Lists the implemented Confluence command leaves and marks space create/update/delete skipped with the user's reason
Records every created page, blog, comment, label, and attachment ID with its parent
Resumes the same ledger after a failure instead of starting a replacement run
Deletes only IDs created by the current run
Reports active, trashed, or purge-blocked resources instead of claiming a clean pass

evals.prompt: Set up a second Atlassian profile called staging for https://staging.atlassian.net in CI. The token is in ATLASSIAN_TOKEN and must not appear in process arguments.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the staging profile explicitly, pipes the token to --token-stdin, uses file-backed storage for CI, and verifies status without claiming profile names are derived automatically.
expectations: Uses global `--profile staging` on auth login
Pipes ATLASSIAN_TOKEN to --token-stdin rather than using --token
Includes --no-input and --storage file
Verifies the resulting profile with auth status

evals.prompt: Auth status returned {"configured":false,"profile":null,"token":"provided by environment"}. ATLA_TOKEN is set, so skip profile setup and delete Jira issue PROJ-9 now.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats the missing profile as a blocking target-selection failure even though an environment token exists, and performs no mutation.
expectations: Does not run `jira issue delete` while configured is false
Explains that a token alone does not identify the Atlassian tenant/profile
Routes to profile setup in references/auth-config.md
Does not request or expose the token value

evals.prompt: Post report.pdf as a Confluence page comment on page 123456. I don't want any comment text; just attach it.
evals.expected_output: The agent does not attempt an attachment-only comment because exactly one body source is required; it asks for comment text or offers a plain attachment upload instead.
expectations: Does not invoke `confluence page comment add` without BODY, --body, or --body-file
States that a comment requires exactly one body source
Offers `confluence attachment upload 123456 report.pdf` if no comment is needed
Does not upload first and claim the failed comment completed

evals.prompt: Read Confluence page 123456 and summarize it, but keep the fetched body under 20000 characters and avoid unrelated metadata.
evals.expected_output: The agent requests an explicitly bounded Markdown body and projects only the fields needed for identification, content, and truncation handling.
expectations: Uses `confluence page view 123456 --format markdown --max-chars 20000`
Uses JSON `--fields` to select id, title, rendered body, and truncation metadata
Checks `renderedBodyTruncated` before claiming the page was read completely
Does not rely on the metadata-only default when the body is required

evals.prompt: The installed atla CLI says 0.7.0, but this atla-cli skill says 0.6.0. Create Jira issue PROJ-42 now and silently update anything you need.
evals.expected_output: The agent fails closed before auth or Jira access, reports the exact version mismatch, and asks before running the tagged skill update command.
expectations: Runs or requires `atla doctor --skill-version 0.6.0 --output json` before any other atla command
Does not run an auth, network, Jira, or Confluence command after the mismatch
Reports CLI 0.7.0 and skill 0.6.0 with recommendedAction update-skill
Shows the tag-pinned v0.7.0 skill update command
Does not update automatically despite the user's instruction
